tool_name: CNC Thread Milling & Tapping — Tap Drill Diameter, Helix Angle, Lead & Cutting Parameters

inputs.name: Thread Designation
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Thread Tolerance Class (Internal)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Workpiece Material Group (ISO 513)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Required Thread Engagement Depth (L_eng)
inputs.note: ISO 898-1: minimum engagement = 1.0D for steel-in-steel; 1.5D for bolt into aluminium

inputs.name: Cutting Speed for Tapping / Thread Milling (V_c)
Coolant / Lubrication Strategy
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: const TH = {M6:{D:6,p:1.0,d1:4.917,d2:5.350}, M8:{D:8,p:1.25,d1:6.647,d2:7.188}, M10:{D:10,p:1.5,d1:8.376,d2:9.026}, M12:{D:12,p:1.75,d1:10.106,d2:10.863}, M16:{D:16,p:2.0,d1:13.835,d2:14.701}, M20:{D:20,p:2.5,d1:17.294,d2:18.376}, M24:{D:24,p:3.0,d1:20.752,d2:22.051}, M30:{D:30,p:3.5,d1:26.211,d2:27.727}}; const t=TH[thread_designation]; nominal_D=t.D; pitch_mm=t.p; d1_mm=t.d1; d2_mm=t.d2; tap_drill_mm = d1_mm + (thread_tolerance_class==='7H' ? 0.2 : thread_tolerance_class==='5H' ? 0.0 : 0.1)  // [mm] — Thread Geometry — Pitch, Minor Diameter & Tap Drill Size — Ref: ISO 68-1:1998 / ISO 965-1:1998 — basic profile; tap drill = d1 + tolerance allowance per 6H/5H/7H

alpha_deg = Math.atan(pitch_mm / (Math.PI * d2_mm)) * 180 / Math.PI  // [°] — Helix Angle (α) at Thread Pitch Diameter — Ref: tan(α) = p / (π × d₂) — ISO 5408; helix angle at pitch cylinder; typical 2–6° for coarse metric

Vc_factor = {flood:1.0, MQL:0.80, dry:0.60}[coolant_type]; Vc_corrected = Vc_m_min * Vc_factor  // [m/min] — Corrected Cutting Speed for Coolant Strategy — Ref: Coolant reduction factors — Sandvik C-2920 §12.4; dry threading requires significant speed reduction
n_rpm = Vc_corrected * 1000 / (Math.PI * nominal_D)  // [rpm] — Spindle Speed for Tapping / Thread Milling (n) — Ref: n = V_c × 1000 / (π × D) — applied to nominal thread diameter
Vf_mm_min = n_rpm * pitch_mm  // [mm/min] — Synchronised Feed Rate (V_f) — Rigid Tapping — Ref: V_f = n × p — rigid tapping synchronisation; F_per_rev = p (exact); CNC G84 / G74 cycle

const Vc_mult = {P:1.0, M:1.8, K:0.7, N:0.4, S:2.5}[workpiece_material_group]; Mc_Nm = 0.00022 * Vc_mult * Math.pow(nominal_D, 2.6) * pitch_mm  // [N·m] — Cutting Torque Estimate (M_c) & Spindle Load Check — Ref: Approximate tapping torque: M_c = C_m × D^2.6 × p; C_m empirical per material group — Titex / Walter reference data

rules.message: Tap drill diameter > 95% of nominal: insufficient thread material — tap drill calculation error; verify thread pitch
Thread engagement < 0.8D in non-steel material: strip-out risk — increase engagement to minimum 1.5D for aluminium, 2.0D for plastics
High speed on large tap (D > 10 mm): cutting speed is aggressive — verify machine spindle torque rating at this RPM; tap breakage risk increases significantly

smart_warnings.message: Dry tapping in stainless steel: tap seizure imminent — stainless work-hardens rapidly; mandatory MQL or flood coolant; reduce Vc to ≤ 8 m/min
Cutting speed too high for superalloy threading: V_c > 6 m/min in Ni/Ti alloys causes rapid BUE and tap fracture — reduce to 3–5 m/min and use HSSE-PM or solid carbide tap
Very small helix angle (< 2°): fine pitch thread — chip evacuation critical; use spiral flute tap (right-hand helix) for blind holes to eject chips upward
High feed rate on small tap: synchronisation error of ±1 rpm causes proportionally large pitch error — verify CNC rigid tapping encoder resolution; use compensation chuck below M8
Deep blind hole threading: chip accumulation risk — use spiral flute tap and retract every 5 mm for chip evacuation; flood coolant at maximum pressure mandatory

fmea.effect: Tap drill diameter > 95% of nominal: insufficient thread material — tap drill calculation error; verify thread pitch
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Thread engagement < 0.8D in non-steel material: strip-out risk — increase engagement to minimum 1.5D for aluminium, 2.0D for plastics
High speed on large tap (D > 10 mm): cutting speed is aggressive — verify machine spindle torque rating at this RPM; tap breakage risk increases significantly

fmea.failureMode: Dry tapping in stainless steel: tap seizure imminent — stainless work-hardens rapidly; mandatory MQL or flood coolant; reduce Vc to ≤ 8 m/min
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'M' && coolant_type === 'dry'

fmea.failureMode: Cutting speed too high for superalloy threading: V_c > 6 m/min in Ni/Ti alloys causes rapid BUE and tap fracture — reduce to 3–5 m/min and use HSSE-PM or solid carbide tap
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: workpiece_material_group === 'S' && Vc_m_min > 6

fmea.failureMode: Very small helix angle (< 2°): fine pitch thread — chip evacuation critical; use spiral flute tap (right-hand helix) for blind holes to eject chips upward
High feed rate on small tap: synchronisation error of ±1 rpm causes proportionally large pitch error — verify CNC rigid tapping encoder resolution; use compensation chuck below M8
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Vf_mm_min > 500 && nominal_D < 8

fmea.failureMode: Deep blind hole threading: chip accumulation risk — use spiral flute tap and retract every 5 mm for chip evacuation; flood coolant at maximum pressure mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: thread_depth_mm > nominal_D * 2.5
